evals.prompt: I'm developing a new Next.js SaaS application. Can you set up the necessary API configuration so that OpenClaw-based agents can enter the system and create objects?
evals.expected_output: API endpoints, authentication, scopes and tools.json manifest specifically for the domain objects.

assertions.name: includes_discovery_phase
assertions.description: Response must explicitly ask or mention the need to scan/discover the existing domain entities first

assertions.name: includes_idempotency_key
assertions.description: Response must recommend an Idempotency-Key header to prevent duplicate records

assertions.name: includes_tenant_id
assertions.description: Response must require a tenant_id (or org_id / workspace_id) for multi-tenant scoping

assertions.name: includes_tools_json_manifest
assertions.description: Response must include or describe a tools.json agent manifest based on the abstract resource

evals.prompt: We have an internal E-Commerce dashboard. Could you design a tools.json manifest and a '/search' endpoint so that bots can search the system and retrieve results using OpenAI tool-calling?
evals.expected_output: Search endpoint definition with semantic/FT search considerations, rate limiting, and the tools.json manifest search block tailored to E-Commerce.

assertions.name: includes_search_endpoint
assertions.description: Response must define the GET /v1/search endpoint with tenant_id and q parameters

assertions.name: mentions_rate_limiting
assertions.description: Response must recommend rate limiting for the search endpoint

assertions.name: mentions_sql_fts_and_semantic
assertions.description: Response should cover both SQL FTS (MVP) and semantic/embedding search (V2)

assertions.name: includes_tools_manifest_for_search
assertions.description: Response must include an OpenAI tool definition block for searching the specific domain resources

evals.prompt: I want to add a structure to our existing HR application where external AI bots can securely drop inputs. How should I design the API structure using the Inbox/Capture approach?
evals.expected_output: Endpoints for '/v1/captures' along with the required authentication and minimum sufficient scopes.

assertions.name: includes_captures_endpoint
assertions.description: Response must define POST /v1/captures (or drafts) as the safe bot entry point

assertions.name: uses_write_captures_scope
assertions.description: Response must recommend the 'write:captures' scope (not direct write access) for bot credentials

assertions.name: explains_human_review_step
assertions.description: Response must explain the human review / convert-to-resource step in the inbox workflow

assertions.name: includes_source_agent_field
assertions.description: Response must include source, agent_name, or trace_id for traceability

evals.prompt: A bot is calling our API without a tenant_id. What error should we return and how should the response be structured?
evals.expected_output: Standard error format with code 'validation_error' and a descriptive message.

assertions.name: uses_standard_error_format
assertions.description: Response must show the standard error envelope: { error: { code, message, details } }

assertions.name: uses_validation_error_code
assertions.description: Response must use the 'validation_error' error code for a missing required field

assertions.name: suggests_api_consistency
assertions.description: Response should recommend consistent error formatting across all endpoints

evals.prompt: Our bot's API key got leaked. What scope model and API design decisions would have limited the damage?
evals.expected_output: Scope isolation, principle of least privilege, delete/bulk scopes disabled by default, audit logging.

assertions.name: mentions_least_privilege_scopes
assertions.description: Response must emphasize granting only required scopes (e.g. write:captures, not write:[resource])

assertions.name: mentions_delete_scope_isolation
assertions.description: Response must recommend that write:[resource]:delete is explicitly isolated and off by default for bots

assertions.name: mentions_audit_logging
assertions.description: Response must recommend audit logs with actor/action/trace_id for breach detection
